text: Large language models, or LLMs, are advanced computer programs that understand and generate human-like text. They work by learning patterns from massive amounts of written data, such as books, articles, and websites. During training, they process this data to predict words and sentences, gradually building a complex understanding of language. Users interact with LLMs through prompts—specific questions or instructions that guide the model’s response. This is called prompt engineering. LLMs can write essays, answer questions, and even create poetry. However, they have limits. They may produce incorrect or biased information, and they lack real understanding or common sense. They simply generate text based on what they have learned.
